SYNTHETIC NOTE. Not a real patient. Structure imitates an EHR export for
testing note ingestion and normalization. No real PHI. Contains a synthetic
MRN and DOB label only to exercise the (non-blocking) medium PHI findings.

======================================================================
Springfield Orthopedic & Spine Associates
Office Visit Note - Established Patient
======================================================================
Patient: Testcase, Jane (SYNTHETIC)        MRN: SYN000123
DOB: 01/01/1975                            Sex: F
Encounter Date: 06/01/2026                 Provider: A. Clinician, MD
Department: Pain Medicine
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Chief Complaint:
Low back pain radiating to the left leg.

HPI:
Ms. Testcase is a 51 y/o established patient who presents with an 8-week
history of low back pain radiating into the left posterior thigh and calf.
The pain is described as sharp and burning, worse with prolonged sitting
and forward flexion, and partially relieved by rest. She denies saddle
anesthesia, bowel or bladder incontinence, fever, chills, or unexplained
weight loss.

Conservative Care to Date:
- Physical therapy: completed 6 weeks, minimal improvement.
- Medications: naproxen with partial relief.
- Injections: none to date.

Review of Systems:
Constitutional: Negative for fever, negative for weight loss.
Neurologic: Positive for left leg radicular pain. Negative for saddle
anesthesia.
All other systems reviewed and negative.

Physical Exam:
Vitals: BP 128/78, HR 72, Temp 98.4 F.
Back: Paraspinal tenderness, left lumbar region.
Neuro: Positive straight leg raise on the left at 40 degrees. Diminished
sensation in the left L5 dermatome. Strength 4/5 left extensor hallucis
longus. Reflexes 2+ and symmetric.

Assessment:
Lumbar radiculopathy, left L5 distribution, persistent despite 6 weeks of
conservative therapy.

Plan:
Order lumbar MRI without contrast to evaluate for disc herniation or nerve
root compression given failure of conservative management. Discussed risks,
benefits, and alternatives with the patient, who wishes to proceed.

Electronically signed by A. Clinician, MD on 06/01/2026.
